text: A well-designed testing strategy is crucial for building reliable applications. Unit tests verify small, individual parts of the code, such as functions or methods. Meanwhile, integration tests ensure that different modules work together correctly as a whole. Many developers use test-driven development (TDD), which means writing tests before writing the actual code. This approach helps identify problems early and leads to cleaner, more thoughtful designs. Ultimately, a strong test suite gives you confidence. When you add new features or make changes, you can be sure that your existing code still works. This saves time, reduces bugs, and makes the development process smoother and more predictable.
